milestone: Scene Decoration Images — the Real Root Cause, Found With the Diagnostic That Just Shipped
slice: A direct, same-session follow-up: the Decoration Image diagnostic warning added moments earlier surfaced the real cause the instant the user checked their own browser console -- a Studio Cover page's "Paper BG" image was being requested as a bare, unresolved relPath (scenes/cover/decoration.png) directly against Studio's own page origin, never resolved against the compiled Theme's own assets map at all. Root cause, traced to its actual source: externalizeSceneImage(scene,layer,package_) in tools/world-builder-v2/js/services/builder.js resolved a Scene Layer's vihu-asset: image reference via AssetStore.resolve(src), then only embedded the result into package_.assets if it happened to start with data: -- but AssetStore.resolve() deliberately never returns a data: URI for a vihu-asset: reference (it resolves to a warm blob: object URL or a signed Storage https: URL, by design, per docs/DRAFT_ASSET_ARCHITECTURE.md), so that check has silently failed for EVERY real Scene Decoration image uploaded since Draft Asset Architecture's Phase B shipped, while the function kept returning the relPath as if externalization had succeeded regardless. Confirmed tools/world-builder/ (v1) has no equivalent bug -- it never produces vihu-asset: references at all. Fixed by resolving through AssetStore.hydrateForExport(src) instead -- the module's own existing, already-tested inverse of put(), purpose-built for exactly this (resolve a ref straight back to a real, durable, embeddable data: URI, checking the local IndexedDB blob directly first) -- reused rather than reimplementing its own fetch+FileReader logic a second time. Verified via a new Playwright suite driving the real, unmodified pipeline end to end (a real AssetStore.put()-backed vihu-asset: reference fed into the real convergeSceneLayer()/externalizeSceneImage()), confirmed via git stash to reproduce the exact reported failure on the pre-fix code and to pass cleanly with the fix restored. Both goldenBuild.js suites pass unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
